Hey everyone, this is Russell. I want to welcome you guys to module number two of High-Ticket Secrets. And I apologize for a couple minutes late getting started here. Just had a bunch of technical issues, but we are here and live and excited to share with you guys some really, really cool stuff. So to begin with, before we get started, I just want to ask you guys a quick question. Just see if you all had a chance over the last week or so to kind of start planning out your back-end product. We go over a lot of different examples and ideas and hopefully got the wheels in your head spin. So if you kind of decided what that's going to be or you know what it is you're going to be offering people, let me know in the chat box just so I can get an idea that you guys are keeping up. Good, good. Looks like you guys are getting it. Good. I want to make sure that you guys are keeping up with this. You know, the worst thing that, I've been doing this now for over a decade and the people that are the most successful, those who were like, as we teach something, they implement it as we go versus those who just kind of listen to the whole thing and then they think they're going to come back and implement and they never do. So make sure that each step as we're going through this, that you're kind of creating it and preparing it and getting all those things ready as you go. So today we are going to be talking about the two-step funnel. Next week we're going to be talking about actually how to set up your call center and Robbie's going to come on that one and kind of go over a whole bunch of really cool stuff with you guys. And then the last one we'll be talking about traffic and how we drive traffic to fill our funnels and do a bunch of cool stuff. So that is the game plan for today. Now, last meeting we had, it was interesting. We had a very diverse group that's here. We had a lot of people selling like information coaching with people selling more traditional coaching. We had offline, like offline type businesses. I apologize, my Skype was making some noise so I just logged out. Offline businesses with people who don't have a product, who don't want to have a product. We had people who've got a whole entire product line. So we had a lot of people from a lot of different places which always makes it fun. And so because of that, I'm going to show you guys a couple different ways to do these two-step funnels. And we've had success with all the ways I'm going to be showing you guys. Plus I'm going to talk about a couple ways that we personally haven't done but I have friends that have done and things like that as well. So anyway, yes, someone asked about when the recording for this would be in the members area. I would say tomorrow is the latest, maybe tonight, but tomorrow is the latest. We'll have the recording up. Okay, so that's kind of the game plan. So I'm going to jump right into these. So with the, in fact, I'm going to cut to a picture of Russell talking. That's the next slide. This is for story time. So I decided I'm going to show this picture every time I jump into story time mode. I want to kind of tell you guys how our call center used to run so that you guys first off won't do it. And second off, you'll see the power and what we're going to be talking about now. So the way that it used to work, basically we would sell a lot of front end products, right? Free plus shipping things, $27, $37 products. We would sell these things and then when people would buy them, we would get their phone number on the order form and then we would outbound call them and basically call them up and try to offer them our coaching. And the script that we use is very similar to the one we kind of use now, the one that Robbie trains on in the members area. But that was kind of our model. And when we were doing that, we were averaging about $150 for every lead that we were able to actually get on the phone. Okay, so a lot of times there were bad numbers or international or things that we couldn't get a hold of it. For every single number that we were actually able to hold of, we averaged $150 per lead. Okay, so if we sold the product for $30, it means on average we make another $150 when we call that person on the phone. Okay, not too bad, right? You look at that way, it's like, wow, that's a pretty good return. Especially if you're selling the $27 or $37 product, you're making five or six times as much from the back end. So I was always really excited by that number. Now, the couple of things that I didn't really like what we were doing. One of the biggest problems we had is that we were basically, we were cold calling people, right? They didn't know that they were getting a phone call most of the time. They just, they'd bought something and a little while later we were calling them and talking to them. And that's kind of, you know, that's what it was. And so because of that, there were a lot of people that didn't like that. There was, you know, some pushback from customers and things like that. And I think in reality in the long term, it kind of hurt my business. I don't think, I think a lot of times people wouldn't buy it for me because they didn't want to get that pesky phone call afterwards. But that was kind of what we were doing. That was the model. Now, luckily for you guys, the process I'm showing you guys is a lot better because you don't have any of that. You're not going to be cold calling anybody, which makes it a lot nicer. And you don't get that kind of blowback at all. But that's what we were doing. Now, what we found, in fact, let me open a paintbrush. I can write these numbers down because I want these numbers in your head because it's not just story time. It's actually ties into the numbers that we're averaging now and kind of what you can expect if you kind of follow our process. So we're averaging, like I said, $150 per lead. So the metric that we used back then was called DPL, $1 per lead. So I would call down to our call center each week and say, hey, what's our average DPL right now? They say, hey, I ran our average about $148 DPL. Hey, we're $180 DPL this week. And I would know $180 DPL. We'd sent them 3,000 leads. You kind of could figure out the math based on that. So that was the metric that drove that whole business. Now, somewhere in the interim of us setting this whole thing up, and I can't remember if it was an accident or whatever, but I had this idea. I said, look, every single person who bought something from us, what if we sent them a physical letter in the mail and offer them a free bonus if they would basically pick up the phone and give us a call? So that was my next kind of thought. So everyone that would buy something, we would go out there and we'd ship them a sales letter. And we had this thing that was called our checklist. So I had like six checklists with a CD of me explaining all the checklists. And for free, they just had to call our office and talk to one of our salespeople. We'd ship them out this checklist. And so we put it in there. We started doing it for a while. And I wasn't tracking it all, but I knew that when the inbound calls came in, they would talk to a salesperson and that way we didn't have to try to outbound call them. That was kind of the strategy behind it. And so we ran that for a couple of months. And then actually, because we were paying money on the shipping of these letters after a while, we were like, let's just stop. It's costing us all this money. We don't want to ship them anymore. And then fast forward, literally three or four weeks later, Robbie came up to me. He'd only been working for us for a couple of months. I didn't know him that well at the time. And he said, man, can we, they just call him Pat Lively's because Pat Lively was the phone service that would do the inbound call for us. He said, man, can we get any more, many more of those Pat Lively's. I'm like, what do you mean? He's like, those Pat Lively's are gold, man. We want some more of those leads. I'm like, really? They're gold. He said, yeah, when somebody calls us and we talk to him, like those are like, it's literally gold. I said, really? Well, what's the like, like, let's put some metrics down. It's finally like how good it actually is. And so we went back and we ran all the Pat Lively's, how many deals we had done off and all that kind of stuff. And we found out after running the math that for every single person who initiated the phone call to us, we were making $450 per lead. Okay, $450 if they called us. Something got really excited. And I think there's a lot of psychology behind that part of it, I think is just the fact that if you think about it, like when you're outbound calling somebody, they could be eating dinner. And all of a sudden the phone rings and they pick up the phone. They're kind of an angry state, right? I know in our phone rings when we're dinner, like I usually pick up, I say a couple of words and I hang back up, right? It's not usually a pleasant thing. That's the state I'm entering. Whereas if somebody just sent me a gift, I'm calling them and they're answering the phone to give me this cool thing. What's the state I'm entering that conversation in? It's obviously way different. And so the states that we enter different things in have a lot to do with it. We call this pre-framing in NLP. And we do a lot of pre-framing stuff in ourselves, funnels and things like that. But the concept is you change that frame of how they enter the conversation, it changes the outcome. From $150 per lead to $450. So we started setting up all these different elements in ourselves, funnels, to get people to start calling us. That was like our goal, call us, call us, call us. We started doing that. And so we started just increasing it all across. And these were our goal. This was like the best thing in the world for us. It was called Pat Live. So Pat Live is just a phone system. I think it's patlive.com, P-A-T-L-I-V-E.com. So what they do is they have like a recorded message. They would call that number, that recorded message, and they would ask for the thing. And then our salesperson would call back and just say, hey, got your message. You requested this free thing. We're going to ship it out to you. What's your address? And then boom, they start the sales call. That's kind of how it worked. Cool. So that was like a big aha. So for years, that's how we ran our call center. $150 outbound, 450 inbound. And we just kept running that, running that, running that. And then as you know, as I talked about throughout, we shut that down a few years ago. And then when we kind of restarted this, there were, I was, I think I talked to this. I was very nervous about restarting it for a lot of reasons. First off, I did not want to have 60 salespeople. And I didn't want to have people not like me. I wanted people to buy my product, not be afraid to call me and all these things. And so because of that, like three or four years, we did not ever call any of our customers. And we were losing tons and tons of money until we sat back and kind of thought about how can we tweak this and actually make it work. And so our first initial test, what we did is instead of people buying anything, all we did is, so we had people that had bought our product, right? They had bought something. And then instead of just going out there and calling all these people, we said, look, let's set up a system where they've got to call us instead. Okay, so let's just completely get rid of this right here. Let's just kill it. And it's only focused on these right here. And instead of them calling in and trying to trick them about, you know, come and get your free thing and then do the sales call, like let's make them actually know that we're going to call them to try to sell them something. And that way we're just getting the cream off the top, right? It's interesting. If you look at our old sales force, I'm going to show you how it was kind of set up because this will help you understand the point of what we would do. We would have these cubes, right? And each cube would have, or each, this is a big desk, right? There's a cube there, cube there, cube there. So we'd have like six to eight people sitting in here and there's one person at the top, right? So the other guys were setters and this guy up here was like the big closer, okay? So the closer was in charge of all these people. So what we would do is we would drop leads in here each week. We basically, we needed about 50 leads per person per week. Okay, so if we have eight people, I'll make eight people so we can do it for numbers. So boom, there's 200 leads here, 200 leads here. So we're looking at 400, 400 or so buyer leads a week we needed to make this little thing work. And then we ended up, we had like five or six of these, of these pods, okay? Through our business. So that's kind of how the whole thing worked. And so we had to drive tons and tons of leads come here to give all these people a filter down and come to the closer and that's kind of how the process worked, right? So I was saying like what if we do the same thing? What if we just, we got rid of all these people. We just kept one person here, one person here. Let's get rid of all the people who were outbound calling and it's only focused on these people. But let's put up a whole another hurdle so it's even harder. So the only people that rise to the top are the cream of the crop. And so we had down here, again, here's a product we were selling. And then after somebody buy the product, we would basically say, hey, if you're interested in having help with this product, we can come coach you through it. There's an application you can come and apply to come work with us. This is why we're great and why you should work. Let's come apply. And so people started applying. Now, when we first started doing this, can anybody guess what our dollar per lead was, our dollar per application that we were getting? What's your guys guess? Okay, it surprised me. All right, we got a $900 guess, a $500 guess, a $250 guess. A couple of different guesses here, a $3,000 guess, all right, $1,500, $950. All right, so when we very first did it, when we first started this, we were averaging over, ready for this, boom, over $1,000 per app. Okay, I think we first started close to like $1,200 an app and that fluctuated, dropped down to $900 and $800. But somewhere in there, if you look at as a whole, everything right now were over $1,000. I think this week, for example, I was just talking to Robbie today and he told me that I think as of today, we were at like $850, $850 lead, I believe. But they have like three or four big deals that are about to go through. If even one of those, one of the $25,000 deals go through, then the entire thing across the board will jump up to $1,200, $1,500 somewhere in there. But I would say to be pretty safe for an average, it's over $1,000 per application that's coming through. Okay, you guys getting this? So all of a sudden, I used to have to have 10 guys, 10 times as many leads to make the same amount of money, right? It just, it changed everything for us, okay? And so that's kind of the process. If you do that, what just happened just kind of negates everything that they just, they just did, right? They just, they basically just bought your book. They were convinced it was the greatest thing in the world. It's going to change their life. They made a commitment. They're all excited to read it. And then you're like, oh yeah, you shouldn't read that. Okay, that's not the way you want to position it. You do want to position like, hey, you just bought my course. So I'm asking him for it because I want them to kind of like, I want them to kind of feel dumb like, oh, I don't really have a sales funnel. I kind of do. The weight loss space, even if you're in great shape, you still think you're fat. Okay. That's just how it is. So, so I ask them questions that, that kind of lead to what I know they want, but they're probably not happy with right now. Okay. So I know they want a sales funnel. That's why they're here, but they're probably not happy with theirs right now. So let me just find out like, what do you got? Tell me what you have right now. Okay. It's going to make you feel a little self-conscious. Like, you know, the way Russell does them, there's usually multi-steps and blah, blah, blah, blah, blah. Right. What's the number one problem in your business right now? Again, this is the key thing for sales person. If we know what their problem is, then we can do what? We can help resolve their problem. That's our whole goal, right? Other questions. These questions here are mostly for me to find out how much money they have, to be honest. Okay. So how big is your list? They have no list. They probably don't have any money yet. If they got a big list, they're probably making some money. Okay. This is how I ask how much money they're making without asking how much money they're making. Okay. How much money they have to invest just because that's going to give me a good idea of what we can sell them, right? KX is what are your company's monthly growth sales? Okay. Find out kind of where they're at during the month. They're making no sales. I know that. They're making a thousand bucks, 10,000 bucks a month, making 10,000, making 50 grand a month. I know that somebody's making $50,000 a month. We're probably going to pitch them on our 25 or $100,000 product, not our $8,000 product, right? But if they're making less than 10 grand a month, we're probably going to sell them our $8,000 product. Okay. Just helps me to know kind of where they're at and how big is your advertising budget? In my space, somebody's advertising budget directly correlates with how much money they can invest. Okay. So, it's a direct correlation for me without saying how much money do you have invested in my coaching program? I ask how much money they invest in advertising and that kind of tells me what they're able to invest in a coaching program. Okay. So, this question is magic for me. For your business, it might be different. You just got to think like what things correlate, which things aren't you directly saying, hey, can you afford me? But you know that in your business, they're going to need that kind of cash flow. Where are they spending it anyway? Those are kind of the things that I'm looking for. Okay. All right. This question right here, we got this from, I think I told you guys last week to read this. If not, you should. If you go to Amazon and type in Dan Sullivan, the question, if I spelled that right. Yeah, this book right here. I recommend all you guys invest $20.55 right now by Dan Sullivan question. You can read the book like literally in 30 minutes, but one of the best sales books you will ever find. It makes things very, very simple. Basically, this question comes from that. He's saying, hey, if we're having this discussion three years from today, I think he says one year, but if we're having this discussion one year from today and looking back over the last 12 months, what would have happened in your life, both personally and professionally, for you to feel happy with your progress? Okay. That question is going to get them to future cast and kind of think about their future and what they want and then bring it back. There's so much psychology behind how this question works. You should use it in your, I mean, you're going to be using it in your sales scripts, about a thousand other places, but I like starting with the actual application. So again, this fits in any market, weight loss, finance. If you're selling furniture, it can work. If you're selling cars, it can work. It works for anything. Then I want them to sell themselves on why they should work with me. Okay. This is application only. There's a big way in this. Tell me why you think you'd be a good fit to work with me. Okay. When you feel it can take action now, someday, soon. So I want to find out how serious they are and then fill in the blank. I am an excuse maker. Looks for opportunities, reasons not to take advantage of them. A procrastinator or an action taker. And what's shocking to me still is like half the people still check off excuse maker. If I was applying for something, I would not admit that ever. I would try to put forth my best foot, but people still don't. So those are the questions I ask. I've noticed a lot of people do different questions and it's all good. The reality is the questions aren't nearly as important as just the state that those questions put somebody in. Okay. Where it's starting this positioning process, where it's positioning you as the expert. Okay. All right. So that's the second step here. And then the third. So I actually felt that form. Then we add in this homework page. This homework page is one of the keys. Okay. If I could tell you, it's one piece of this thing that took our average order rate from whatever it was to a thousand and beyond. This homework page is one of the keys. Just it works awesome. Let me see if I can find it again. Too many pages open. So someone asked a question. The last question, if they are excuse maker, an action taker, or an action taker is important. The sales rep responding differently depending on how they put it down. All the questions are important. If you're going to, depending on how many leads you have, you're probably going to call the people that say I'm an action taker a lot faster than you'd call the person that says I'm a procrastinator. So when the reps get their leads each day, it's totally grading. What's our A leads? Our B leads? Our C leads? So the people that are the action takers that actually have money, we're going to call first. And the next set second and third and fourth, you kind of work through there. So those questions are a lot for the sales person to be able to grade who they're calling and what a word and all those types of things. Good question. Okay. So here's the homework page. This homework page is magic. A couple things are going to happen. Now what I want you guys to understand is that this entire application process, I talked to you guys about last week. It's all about like, like positioning yourself as the guru on the hill. No one's going to pay you five or 10 or $25,000 or $100,000 if they don't believe that you're worth it, right? And if they can just pick up the phone and talk to you super easy, they're not going to pay it. If they can get to you super easy, they're not going to pay for it. So you have to have stuff in place to try to position yourself different so that they want to ascend up and talk to you and work through those type of things. And so it's all starting in this process. Now the email sequence you guys get after you subscribe to this is some of the best stuff. In fact, I'm going to pull it up because it's really good stuff. I started moving things to get responses and get responses down for like a week. So we're moving again. But if I can remember how to... Auditor's sponsors... Oh, managed auditor's sponsors. Okay, let me find my auditor's sponsors sequence. I'll read the emails because after they apply before the homework, there's a whole bunch of emails that come out. Okay. Cool. So here's email number one. Okay. Thank you so much for applying for my inner circle. We've received your application and we're looking forward to talking with you. Now here's what to expect next. Okay, and second, in the next day or two, our team is going to be reviewing and pre-approving your applications. In the next 24 to 72 hours, you're going to be hearing from us. No, if you're accepted, then the next 24 to 72 hours, you're beginning a phone call from one of our team members to go over your application. If you're not accepted, you're going to receive an email letting you know that you aren't accepted and what our suggestions are for you to take next. I'm not trying to, uh, to educate them on anything. All I'm trying to do is to build a relationship with them. Okay. Because at this point, if they're going to buy or they're not going to buy, it has nothing to do with how much education I give them or how will I sell it? Very simple funnel. We're not driving a ton of traffic because we don't need to drive a ton of traffic to this. I talked to a student sign up webinar, you know, um, right now one, you know, one sales person needs maybe 20 applications a week. So you got two sales people, maybe need 40 applications a week. So that's all you really need. You don't need more than that. If you get more than that, the, their sales will actually go down. It's funny when I first called, started the call center, we'd give every sales rep like three to 500 leads a day or a week. And, uh, and, uh, we hired some guys that actually had been in call centers before the first thing that came in, they, they took away the 300 leads, gave everyone 50 and first the sales people were so mad. They were angry. They were pissed. And then, um, also they all started making tons more money. So you don't want to give sales people too many leads. Um, it's counterintuitive that you need less and if you have less, they'll work them a lot harder. If you give them 200, they're going to call once and throw it away. Okay. Um, and that's kind of the process guys. It's pretty simple. Okay. It's not that difficult. So that's, that's, this is one, this is one way to do your, your funnel. Okay. I'll show you number two.
